steps.title: Identify supplementary angles from parallel lines
steps.content:
When parallel lines are cut by a transversal, an acute angle and the adjacent obtuse angle are supplementary. They must sum to 180°:
$$5x + 52 + 7x + 92 = 180$$

steps.title: Solve for x and find the angle measures
steps.content:
Combine like terms:
$$12x + 144 = 180$$
$$12x = 36$$
$$x = 3$$
Now find the angle measures:
Acute angle = $5(3) + 52 = 67^{\circ}$
Obtuse angle = $7(3) + 92 = 113^{\circ}$

steps.title: Calculate the sum of 2 acute and 3 obtuse angles
steps.content:
All acute angles in this configuration equal 67°, and all obtuse angles equal 113°. The sum requested is:
$$2(67^{\circ}) + 3(113^{\circ}) = 134^{\circ} + 339^{\circ} = 473^{\circ}$$
